problem_framing.primary_objective: Eliminate Day-1 abandonment and establish persistent AI coaching continuity to unlock retention and compounding user value before scaling acquisition.
critical_constraints: 74–77% Day-1 abandonment means the product has no retention foundation to build on — growth is currently a leaking bucket
AI infrastructure hard-capped at $200/month — every agent interaction must be cost-architected, not just cost-aware
Solo founder carrying product, engineering, design, and BD simultaneously — execution bandwidth is the binding constraint
No persistent agent memory — AI coaching is stateless, making personalization claims structurally false at the session boundary
No behavioral economics layer — engagement loops are absent, leaving habit formation entirely to user willpower
success_definition: Day-1 abandonment reduced from 75%+ to below 40% within one redesigned onboarding cycle
Users reach a defined 'aha moment' (first personalized workout generated and completed) within 5 minutes of signup
Persistent memory layer operational — AI agent recalls user history, preferences, and progress across sessions
AI infrastructure cost remains at or below $200/month at up to 1,000 active users via model tiering and prompt optimization
At least one behavioral retention mechanic (commitment device, streak, or variable reward) measurably improves Day-7 return rate
Solo founder can ship, monitor, and iterate without adding headcount or DevOps overhead

value_pools.description: Overwhelmed by choice — needs a frictionless, guided first workout that feels personal and achievable within minutes of signup
Needs an AI coach that remembers their history and evolves programming intelligently — stateless agents destroy this value entirely
Values a system that adapts to schedule constraints and delivers a ready-to-execute plan without configuration overhead each session
Secondary segment seeking wearable integration and recovery-aware programming — high LTV if platform can ingest and act on biometric signals

core_questions: What is the exact drop-off point in the current onboarding funnel and what is the minimum viable 'aha moment' that must be delivered before that point?
What is the lowest-cost persistent memory architecture (vector store, summarization layer, or structured profile) that keeps AI continuity within the $200/month ceiling?
Which single behavioral economics mechanic — commitment device, loss aversion trigger, or variable reward — has the highest expected impact on Day-7 return rate for this user profile?
How should the AI coaching agent tier its model usage (e.g., GPT-4o-mini for routine interactions, GPT-4o for high-stakes personalization moments) to stay cost-viable at scale?
What is the minimum onboarding data collection required to generate a genuinely personalized first workout without creating form fatigue that drives abandonment?
How does TransformFit credibly differentiate from Fitbod's adaptive strength engine given identical budget and solo-founder constraints — what is the defensible wedge?

capability_gaps.current_state: 74-77% Day-1 abandonment; users never reach a personalized workout before disengaging; no mapped drop-off points or funnel instrumentation
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Users reach a completed personalized workout within 5 minutes of signup; Day-1 abandonment below 40%; funnel has discrete measurable checkpoints
risks: Onboarding form fatigue may replace abandonment at a later funnel stage
Perceived personalization without true data depth undermines trust on return visits
A/B testing capacity is near-zero with a solo founder and no analytics infrastructure

capability_gaps.current_state: AI coaching is fully stateless; no session continuity, no user profile accumulation, no cross-session recall — personalization claims are structurally invalid
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Lightweight persistent memory layer (structured profile + summarization) enables agents to recall goals, history, and preferences across sessions within $200/month ceiling
risks: Vector store costs can spike unpredictably at scale without retrieval budgeting
Memory schema design errors early will require expensive migration later
Supabase row-level security must extend correctly to memory objects to avoid data leakage between users

capability_gaps.current_state: No behavioral economics mechanics present; engagement relies entirely on user willpower; no commitment devices, streaks, loss aversion triggers, or variable reward schedules
capability_gaps.ideal_state: At least one high-impact retention mechanic (commitment device or streak with loss aversion framing) measurably improves Day-7 return rate; mechanic is lightweight to ship
risks: Wrong mechanic selection for target user profile (e.g., gamification alienating busy professionals)
Dark patterns risk if loss aversion is implemented without ethical guardrails
Mechanic effectiveness requires instrumentation to validate — no analytics layer currently exists

capability_gaps.current_state: No model tiering, prompt caching, or batching strategy in place; $200/month ceiling is at structural risk as active users grow; cost-per-interaction is unmodeled
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Model tiering (lightweight model for routine interactions, frontier model for high-stakes personalization moments) keeps AI costs at or below $200/month at 1,000 active users
risks: A single viral growth event could blow the monthly budget before cost controls are in place
Over-optimizing for cost too early degrades coaching quality below the threshold that drives retention
Prompt caching effectiveness varies significantly by LLM provider and requires empirical tuning

capability_gaps.current_state: No defined coach persona, motivational interviewing framework, or dialogue quality standards; coaching interactions are likely generic and tonally inconsistent
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Coach persona with consistent voice, motivational interviewing principles, and contextual guidance scripts that feel personal and emotionally resonant across session types
risks: Poorly designed coach persona erodes trust faster than no coaching at all
Motivational tone mismatch between user segments (beginners vs. biohackers) requires persona flexibility
Without evaluation gates, degraded coaching quality ships silently

capability_gaps.current_state: Workout generation exists but lacks documented progression logic, user history integration, or adaptation signals; differentiation from Fitbod is unclear
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Defensible adaptation engine that evolves programming based on session history, perceived difficulty signals, and schedule constraints — with a clear wedge Fitbod cannot replicate
risks: Fitbod has years of training data advantage that cannot be closed with engineering alone
Adaptation logic bugs silently deliver regressive programs that increase injury risk
Without exercise science grounding, AI-generated programs may be technically legal but physiologically suboptimal

capability_gaps.current_state: Feature prioritization is manual and intuition-driven; no funnel instrumentation, no event tracking, no behavioral signal capture to inform roadmap decisions
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Lightweight analytics layer (event tracking on critical funnel steps, Day-1/Day-7 cohort dashboards) enables evidence-based iteration without adding operational overhead
risks: Without instrumentation, onboarding redesign improvements cannot be attributed or validated
Supabase can serve basic analytics needs but requires schema discipline from the start
Solo founder time cost of analytics setup competes directly with feature shipping bandwidth

capability_gaps.current_state: No clearly articulated defensible wedge versus Fitbod, Noom, or commodity AI fitness apps; value proposition relies on generic 'personalization' claims shared by all competitors
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Single sharp differentiation thesis — likely the combination of emotional coaching continuity plus behavioral retention mechanics — that cannot be replicated by data-only or hardware-dependent competitors
risks: Fitbod or a well-funded competitor could ship persistent memory before TransformFit's MVP redesign ships
Positioning work done before retention is proven is premature and risks wasted messaging investment
Solo founder has no external pressure-test mechanism for strategic assumptions

knowns: Day-1 abandonment rate is 74–77%, confirming a critical onboarding failure before core value delivery
No persistent agent memory exists; AI coaching is fully stateless across sessions
AI infrastructure budget is hard-capped at $200/month with no external funding
Solo founder carries all product, engineering, design, and BD responsibilities simultaneously
Tech stack is confirmed: React, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Supabase, Python backend, likely OpenAI API
Target demographic is health-conscious adults aged 25–45 seeking affordable AI-personalized fitness guidance
No behavioral economics mechanics are currently implemented; retention relies entirely on user willpower
Feature prioritization is manual and intuition-driven with no funnel instrumentation or event tracking
Primary competitors are Fitbod, Noom, Peloton, WHOOP, and MyFitnessPal
Founding date is 2023; company is in MVP redesign stage
No defined coach persona, motivational interviewing framework, or dialogue quality standards exist
No A/B testing infrastructure is currently in place
Supabase provides row-level security and JWT-based authentication
The platform targets the gap between generic fitness apps and expensive personal trainers
Secondary segments include biohackers, self-quantifiers, and wearable device users
critical_unknowns: Exact drop-off point(s) within the current onboarding funnel — is abandonment occurring at signup, goal-setting, equipment input, or first workout generation?
Current number of active or registered users — required to baseline cost-per-interaction modeling and validate any retention improvement claims
Actual average token consumption per AI coaching interaction today — without this, the $200/month ceiling cannot be stress-tested against real usage
Whether any user qualitative feedback or exit survey data exists explaining why Day-1 users abandon
Current onboarding flow step count, time-to-first-workout metric, and form field count — the structural cause of abandonment is unmapped
Whether a 'first personalized workout' has ever been defined as a product milestone or instrumented as an event
Which LLM provider and model tier is currently in use (GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Claude, etc.) and what the current per-call cost structure is
Whether any user data or behavioral signals have been collected historically that could seed a persistent memory schema
What the current Day-7 and Day-30 return rates are — Day-1 abandonment is known but downstream cohort retention is unmeasured
Whether Michael has conducted any user interviews or usability tests with actual target users, and if so what was surfaced
What the current onboarding data collection asks of users — number of fields, input types, and perceived friction points
Whether the workout generation engine has documented progression logic or is purely prompt-driven with no structured algorithm
What Fitbod's specific adaptive algorithm weaknesses are from a real user perspective — required to identify a defensible wedge
Whether any wearable or biometric data integration exists or is architecturally planned
What Michael's current weekly engineering bandwidth is in hours — execution sequencing depends on this constraint being quantified
Whether the platform is web-only, mobile-only, or truly cross-platform, and whether PWA or native mobile is in scope for the redesign
What the current cost per registered user acquisition is, even informally — determines urgency of fixing retention before any paid acquisition
Whether Supabase free tier or paid tier is in use, and what the storage and function invocation limits are relative to projected memory architecture needs

research_tracks.question: What are the precise drop-off points, friction moments, and abandonment triggers in fitness app onboarding flows — and what onboarding patterns correlate with reaching a first 'aha moment' within 5 minutes?

What do real users say about Fitbod, Noom, and AI fitness apps in reviews, Reddit threads, and community forums — specifically around onboarding friction, AI coaching quality, memory/continuity failures, and abandonment reasons?

What is the current state of lightweight persistent memory architectures for AI agents — specifically structured profile plus summarization hybrid patterns — and what are the proven cost envelopes for each approach at 1,000 active users?

Which behavioral economics mechanics — commitment devices, loss aversion framing, variable reward schedules, implementation intentions — have the strongest empirical evidence for Day-7 retention improvement in digital health and fitness contexts for adults aged 25–45?

What are the current OpenAI, Anthropic, and open-source LLM pricing structures for model tiering strategies — specifically cost-per-token differentials between frontier and mini models — and what prompt caching and batching mechanisms are available to stay within a $200/month ceiling at 1,000 active users?

What does the existing TransformFit onboarding flow look like in its current state — step count, data fields, time-to-first-workout, and where the AI coaching interaction first surfaces — and where does the funnel structurally break before value delivery?

What are the documented weaknesses and unmet user needs in Fitbod's adaptive workout engine, coaching continuity, and emotional engagement — specifically gaps that a persistent-memory AI coaching agent could exploit as a defensible wedge?

What are the proven minimum viable onboarding data collection patterns in AI fitness and health apps — specifically what is the fewest input fields required to generate a genuinely personalized first workout without triggering form fatigue and abandonment?

data_layers_needed: Current onboarding funnel step map with drop-off rates at each discrete checkpoint — required before any redesign work begins
Real user session recordings or heatmaps from the existing onboarding flow — even a small sample of 10–20 sessions would unlock structural diagnosis
Actual LLM API call logs with token counts, model used, and cost-per-interaction — required to build the cost architecture model
Day-1, Day-7, and Day-30 cohort retention curves for all registered users to date — baseline for measuring any redesign impact
Existing user profile data schema — what is currently stored per user and what gaps exist relative to a persistent memory architecture
Any prior user interview transcripts, support messages, or informal feedback Michael has collected — raw signal for abandonment cause hypothesis
Fitbod, Noom, and top AI fitness app App Store review corpora filtered for onboarding, memory, coaching continuity, and abandonment mentions — competitive gap map
Supabase current usage metrics — storage consumption, function invocation counts, and tier limits relative to projected memory architecture storage needs
Workout generation prompt templates currently in use — required to assess token efficiency and identify compression or caching opportunities
Michael's current weekly engineering hours available for product work — the execution sequencing model depends on bandwidth quantification
temporary_assumptions: Assuming the primary abandonment cliff is at or before the first AI-generated workout is displayed — not post-workout — until internal funnel data confirms otherwise

Assuming OpenAI GPT-4o-mini is the viable primary model tier for routine interactions at approximately $0.15 per 1M input tokens, with GPT-4o reserved for high-stakes personalization moments, pending cost model validation

Assuming a structured JSON user profile stored in Supabase plus a rolling session summarization approach is the lowest-complexity persistent memory architecture within the $200/month ceiling — to be stress-tested against vector store alternatives

Assuming the streak-plus-loss-aversion mechanic is the highest-expected-value behavioral retention intervention for the 25–42 age segment based on existing digital health literature — pending scientific literature review confirmation
Assuming current onboarding collects more than 5 data fields and that reducing to 3 goal-oriented inputs will measurably reduce form fatigue — requires internal asset review to validate
Assuming Michael has zero existing analytics instrumentation beyond what Supabase logs by default — treating event tracking as a greenfield build
Assuming the platform is currently web-first with a React frontend and that mobile PWA is the lowest-cost path to mobile coverage without a native app build
Assuming no wearable API integrations exist and that biohacker segment needs should be deferred past the P0 onboarding and memory fixes to avoid scope creep

Assuming Fitbod's primary exploitable weakness is the absence of emotional coaching continuity and motivational relationship-building — a data-only adaptation engine that does not remember who you are emotionally

Assuming Michael has fewer than 20 hours per week of net engineering execution bandwidth after product, design, and business responsibilities — sequencing plan will need to accommodate this constraint

decision_brief.recommended_path: Evidence-First Parallel Sprint: close the four critical unknowns (funnel drop-off map, LLM cost baseline, memory architecture options, behavioral mechanic evidence) within 72 hours, then execute a tightly sequenced three-workstream redesign sprint anchored on onboarding compression, persistent memory MVP, and a single retention mechanic — all scoped to solo-founder shipping bandwidth and the $200/month hard ceiling.

options.name: Evidence-First Parallel Sprint (Recommended)
options.tradeoff: Spends 3-5 days on targeted evidence gathering before any engineering or design work begins. Eliminates the risk of redesigning the wrong funnel stage or architecting a memory layer that blows the cost ceiling. Slightly delays first shipped improvement but dramatically increases probability that the first shipped improvement actually moves the metric.
options.when_to_choose: When the exact abandonment cliff is unknown, the LLM cost model is unvalidated, and shipping the wrong thing first would consume the solo founder's entire sprint capacity on a non-needle-moving output. This is the current state. Choose this path.

options.name: Immediate Onboarding Redesign Blitz
options.tradeoff: Ships a compressed onboarding redesign within 7-10 days based on current assumptions. Moves fast but risks redesigning a funnel stage that is not the actual abandonment cliff. If the drop-off is at workout generation rather than goal-setting forms, a form compression sprint is wasted. No memory architecture means the 'aha moment' still evaporates after session one.
options.when_to_choose: Only if Michael already has session recording data or a funnel step map that confirms exactly where users are dropping off and has high confidence the fix is form reduction. Not recommended given current evidence gaps.

options.name: Memory Architecture First
options.tradeoff: Prioritizes building the persistent memory layer before touching onboarding. Solves the stateless AI problem structurally but does nothing to get users past the Day-1 cliff. If 75% of users never return after session one, a memory system has near-zero users to serve. Technically sound but strategically backwards given the abandonment severity.
options.when_to_choose: Only after Day-1 abandonment is reduced below 50% and there is a meaningful cohort of returning users for whom memory continuity becomes the next value-unlocking constraint.

options.name: Single Retention Mechanic Fast Ship
options.tradeoff: Picks one behavioral mechanic (streak with loss aversion framing) and ships it in under a week. Very low engineering cost. But retention mechanics applied to a broken onboarding funnel will not save users who never reach the first workout. Mechanic fires on Day-2 behavior — if Day-1 is broken, Day-2 never happens. High effort for low leverage at this stage.
options.when_to_choose: Activate as workstream three, in parallel with onboarding redesign, not before it. The mechanic should be designed now and shipped the moment onboarding delivers a user to session completion.

prioritized_workstreams: WS-0 [Days 1-4]: Evidence Sprint — internal funnel teardown, LLM cost log audit, behavioral mechanic literature pull, competitive onboarding teardown (Fitbod, Noom). Goal: close the four critical unknowns before a single line of redesign code is written.

WS-1 [Days 5-18]: Onboarding Compression and Aha Moment Delivery — reduce to maximum 3 goal-oriented inputs, inject first AI-generated personalized workout within 5 minutes of signup, define and instrument the aha moment event, establish minimum 5-event funnel tracking in Supabase.

WS-2 [Days 5-18, parallel with WS-1]: Persistent Memory MVP — implement structured JSON user profile schema in Supabase with rolling session summarization layer, wire agent context injection on session open, validate cost envelope against $200/month ceiling at projected 1,000 active user scale.

WS-3 [Days 14-25]: Behavioral Retention Mechanic Ship — deploy single highest-evidence mechanic (streak plus loss aversion notification) triggered immediately after first workout completion, instrument Day-7 return event as the primary validation metric.

WS-4 [Days 20-30]: Coach Persona and Dialogue Quality Gate — define AI coach voice, apply motivational interviewing framing to onboarding dialogue and first-session coaching script, run quality review against all agent-facing copy before WS-1 changes go live to all new users.

WS-5 [Post Day-30, conditional]: Competitive Wedge Articulation and Positioning — activate only after Day-1 abandonment is measurably below 50% and at least one cohort has hit Day-7 with the new retention mechanic active. Premature positioning is a distraction at this stage.

WS-6 [Post Day-30, conditional]: Adaptive Workout Engine Differentiation — document and harden progression logic, define the defensible wedge versus Fitbod around emotional coaching continuity plus memory, scope wearable integration only after core retention is validated.
execution_risks: CRITICAL: Solo founder bandwidth collapse — the recommended path assigns 10 agents and 6 workstreams, but all synthesis, decision-making, and shipping falls on Michael. Every agent output must arrive as a ready-to-act spec, not a document requiring further analysis. Agents must produce executable artifacts, not research reports.

CRITICAL: Assumption invalidation cascade — if the internal funnel teardown (WS-0) reveals the abandonment cliff is post-workout rather than pre-workout, the entire WS-1 onboarding compression sprint pivots. All downstream workstream timelines shift. Michael must treat WS-0 output as a hard gate before committing engineering hours to WS-1.

HIGH: Memory architecture cost spike — a structured profile plus summarization approach is assumed safe within $200/month, but this has not been stress-tested against actual token volumes. A single design error in the summarization trigger frequency could make the memory layer prohibitively expensive before any cost guardrail fires. Nova and ledger must produce a cost model with kill-switch thresholds before any memory code ships.

HIGH: Aha moment definition ambiguity — 'first personalized workout generated and completed' is the stated target, but 'completed' is undefined. Does it mean the user viewed the workout? Started the first exercise? Logged completion? Without a precise, instrumentable definition agreed before WS-1 begins, the success metric is unmeasurable and the redesign has no validation anchor.

HIGH: Behavioral mechanic misfit for user segment — streak mechanics are well-evidenced for general populations but can feel punitive or gamification-inappropriate for busy professionals in the 28-42 segment. If the initial user cohort skews professional rather than beginner, the mechanic may produce neutral or negative Day-7 results. Freya must segment the evidence review by user archetype, not just age range.

MEDIUM: Funnel instrumentation bootstrapping problem — pulse needs at least 5 key events instrumented before any redesign ships to establish a pre-redesign baseline. If instrumentation ships after the redesign, there is no counterfactual. This is a sequencing risk that Michael must enforce: instrument first, redesign second, even if it costs 2-3 days.

MEDIUM: Supabase tier limits for memory architecture — the current Supabase tier is unknown. A persistent memory schema with per-user summarization objects and vector embeddings could hit free tier storage or function invocation limits before reaching 1,000 users. Knowledge-engineer must audit current Supabase usage and tier limits as part of WS-0 before memory schema is designed.

MEDIUM: Coach persona inconsistency across workstreams — if WS-1 ships onboarding dialogue before WS-4 defines the coach persona, the voice established in onboarding will be inconsistent with the voice designed in WS-4. Mira must review WS-1 onboarding copy before it ships, even if WS-4 has not formally completed. A voice draft is required as a WS-1 dependency, not a WS-4 output.

LOW: Premature positioning activation — if early WS-1 metrics look promising, there will be pressure to begin marketing and positioning work before retention is validated. Steve and compass are scoped as post-Day-30 conditional activations. Activating them before Day-7 return rate data exists is a distraction risk that could pull Michael's attention from engineering execution at the highest-leverage moment.

LOW: Fitbod competitive response — Fitbod or a well-funded competitor could ship persistent memory or emotional coaching continuity features during TransformFit's redesign window. This risk is real but unactionable at this stage. The correct response is to move faster on the memory plus coaching wedge, not to pause and monitor competitors.
first_30_day_focus: Day 1-2: Michael pulls all available internal data — Supabase user event logs, any API call logs with token counts, current onboarding step list, and any informal user feedback — and delivers to app-experience-studio and nova as the WS-0 evidence package. This is the single highest-leverage action Michael can take before any agent work begins.

Day 1-4: app-experience-studio and marcus execute competitive onboarding teardowns of Fitbod and Noom, mapping step counts, field counts, time-to-first-value, and identified friction patterns. Output must be a ranked friction map, not a narrative report.

Day 1-4: nova and ledger audit current LLM provider, model tier, and per-call cost structure. Produce a cost model: interactions per user per day multiplied by estimated token consumption multiplied by model tier cost equals monthly ceiling stress test at 100, 500, and 1,000 active users. Output must include a hard budget allocation per interaction type.

Day 1-4: freya pulls behavioral economics literature specifically for digital health and fitness app Day-7 retention. Output must be a ranked mechanic shortlist with effect size estimates by user archetype — not a general behavioral economics primer.

Day 3-4: pulse defines the minimum viable instrumentation schema — maximum 7 events covering signup, goal input completion, first workout generated, first workout started, first workout completed, Day-1 session end, and Day-7 return. Michael instruments these events in Supabase before any redesign code ships.

Day 5: WS-0 synthesis gate — Michael reviews all evidence outputs and makes three binary decisions: (1) confirmed abandonment cliff location, (2) confirmed memory architecture pattern, (3) confirmed single retention mechanic to ship. No workstream 1, 2, or 3 work begins until these three decisions are locked.

Day 6-18: mira drafts AI coach voice guide (2-page maximum — persona name, tone spectrum, 10 example response pairs across emotional states) as a WS-1 dependency. This must exist before any onboarding dialogue is written.

Day 6-18: app-experience-studio delivers compressed onboarding wireframes (maximum 3 goal-input screens plus immediate AI workout generation screen). Marcus reviews for friction. Michael implements. Pulse validates instrumentation is firing correctly on first 10 test sessions before broader rollout.

Day 6-18: nova and knowledge-engineer deliver persistent memory schema design (Supabase JSON profile structure plus summarization trigger logic plus session context injection pattern) as a ready-to-implement spec. Michael implements memory layer against this spec, not against a whiteboard design.

Day 14-25: freya delivers retention mechanic implementation spec — not a recommendation document but a literal product spec including trigger condition, notification copy in the coach voice, streak display component requirements, and loss aversion framing language. Michael ships this mechanic to fire on first workout completion.

Day 25-30: first cohort analysis — pulse and compass review Day-1 and Day-7 cohort data from the redesigned onboarding. If Day-1 abandonment has moved below 50%, proceed to WS-5 and WS-6 activation planning. If it has not moved, return to the funnel teardown with new behavioral data and identify the remaining friction source before any further feature work.
